texto: The library in summer is the most wonderful thing because there you get books on any subject and read them each for only as long as they hold your interest, abandoning any that don't, halfway or a quarter of the way through if you like, and store up all that knowledge in the happy corners of your mind for your own self and not to show off how much you know or spit it back at your teacher on a test paper.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Polly Horvath: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The library in summer is the most wonderful thing because there you get books on any subject and …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, books, libraries pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de libraries e reading.

texto: At heart, we're all violent raging wolves, but in our actions we can be pacifists.
explicacao: Citação de Polly Horvath sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “At heart, we're all violent raging wolves, but in our actions we can be pacifists.” articula amor, actions, choices em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como choices e paz.

texto: And Mrs. Treaclebunny has promised to speak English from now on as well. In fact, she said when she goes to England, that's all she speaks anyway because the animals speak English there. She says anyone who has read children's books with animals in them set in England would know that. Is The Wind in the Willows written in Mole with a little Ratty thrown in? Is Winnie-the-Pooh written in Bear? No, it's English, because that's what the animals there speak. I didn't know that before. Travel is so broadening.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Polly Horvath que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “And Mrs. Treaclebunny has promised to speak English from now on as well. In fact, she said when s…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, animals, speak, com foco em viagem e reflexao.

texto: You can be sunk low or as a skunk and still have a joy in your heart. Joy lives like one of those spinning things---a gyroscope in your heart. It doesn't seem to have any connection to circumstance, good or bad.

texto: You can't replace one dog with another any more than you can replace one person with another, but that's not to say you shouldn't get more dogs and people in your life.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Polly Horvath sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “You can't replace one dog with another any more than you can replace one person with another, but…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, dogs, replace, útil em momentos de reflexao.

texto: We all belong here equally...Just by being born onto the earth we are accepted and the earth supports us. We don't have to be especially good. We don't have to accomplish anything. We don't even have to be healthy.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Polly Horvath sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. texto: I keep staring at the bag in Mrs. Parks's hand: yellows, greens, blues, whites, pastel colors so soft they look as if they have faded in the sea. The washed colors of the sea and sleep. Pajama colors. The colors of baby clothes. In my nose is the smell of my brothers' heads after they are born. Maybe this is why people making journeys buy saltwater taffy. It gives you the lovely dreamy sense that you can start all over again from the beginning.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Polly Horvath: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I keep staring at the bag in Mrs. Parks's hand: yellows, greens, blues, whites, pastel colors so …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, baby, beginning pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e beginning.

frase_original: I keep staring at the bag in Mrs. Parks's hand: yellows, greens, blues, whites, pastel colors so soft they look as if they have faded in the sea. The washed colors of the sea and sleep. Pajama colors. The colors of baby clothes. In my nose is the smell of my brothers' heads after they are born. Maybe this is why people making journeys buy saltwater taffy. It gives you the lovely dreamy sense that you can start all over again from the beginning.

texto: There's something about sports. You can be setting fire to cats and burying them in your backyard, but as long as you're playing team sports, people think you're okay.
